page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dldsv7 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server includes 5669 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable. A key architectural tradeoff of the D96lds_v7 is its low memory-to-core ratio, which prioritizes raw compute capacity over memory allocation. It does not include integrated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96lds_v7 offers a high density of dedicated compute cores and substantial local storage capacity, making it suitable for workloads that require significant processing power and local disk space without demanding large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include compute-heavy batch processing, local database hosting, and distributed file systems.
description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor, paired with 192.0 GB of system memory. This configuration results in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, prioritizing compute density. The server is equipped with 5669 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it lacks integrated GPU accelerators and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the hardware profile offers a cost-efficient option for compute-intensive tasks that require substantial local storage but do not demand high memory capacity. Suitable workloads include compute-heavy batch processing, distributed file systems, and local database hosting.
